transcript: Thank you for watching. The Bharat Bandh called by the opposition has evoked mixed response. The call for the bandh has been given in protest against Centre's decision on diesel price hike and allowing FDI in multi-branch retail. While protesters disrupted rail and road traffic in parts of Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, West Bengal and Odisha, Bandh had little impact in Mumbai. In Uttar Pradesh, bandh called by the opposition parties, including the ruling Samantwadi party, has affected normal functioning of day-to-day life in the state. In the national capital, Delhi, most of the markets are closed, but vehiculate movement is normal, though protesters block traffic at some places. A protest rally in support of the band was addressed by NDA convener Sharad Yadav and BJP president Nitin Gadkari in New Delhi. Reiterating its demand for a rollback of free civil rights, the incident changed the current situation, tapping the black line between the NDA convener Sharad Yadav and BJP president Nitin Gadkari in New Delhi............................... Thank you for watching! We'll be right back. To be continued... Thank you for watching. We'll be right back. Sous-titrage Société Radio-Canada Thanks for watching!
